claim_text: The robots.txt convention is widely interpreted as governing whether an LLM may train on the content of a site.

span.selector_value: This document specifies and extends the "Robots Exclusion Protocol"
span.exact_text: This document specifies and extends the "Robots Exclusion Protocol" originally defined by Martijn Koster in 1994 [ROBOTSTXT], with a method that allows service owners to control how content served by their services may be accessed, if at all, by automatic clients known as crawlers.
span.surrounding_context: RFC 9309 Section 1 — Introduction. The RFC scopes robots.txt to crawler access, not training-data permissions.

notes: The RFC defines robots.txt as a crawl-control mechanism. Public discourse conflates it with training-data permission, but the spec does not support that interpretation.
